suggestions: Align content with Module 5 focus on signals - include signal-related system calls (kill, sigaction, sigprocmask, etc.) with detailed explanations
Add hard MCQs: scenario-based questions on return values, error handling, race conditions, or comparing signal APIs
Include code examples demonstrating system call usage in practice
Add numerical problems or trace-through exercises showing process creation with fork/exec
Expand with formal definitions, theorems where applicable, and academic references

rating.relevance_remarks: Content focuses on system calls rather than specific system functions listed in sibling topics (e.g., abort, alarm). However, system calls are foundational to system functions.
rating.coverage_remarks: Good overview of system call categories but lacks depth on specific functions from sibling topics (e.g., no coverage of alarm/pause functions or daemon-related calls).
rating.quality_remarks: Generally accurate but incomplete sections (e.g., Protection System Calls cut off) and MCQ explanation is truncated. Flashcard is well-structured.
rating.tier_remarks: Needs more application-focused content and harder MCQs. Missing proofs/implementation details expected at this tier. Language is appropriately technical but depth insufficient.
suggestions: Complete truncated sections (e.g., Protection System Calls) and MCQ explanations
Add coverage of sibling-topic related functions (e.g., alarm()/pause() in process control)
